applyTo: '**'

# 02 — Build and Run

> Cross-reference: `00-project-overview.md` for repo map, `11-configuration-and-metadata.md` for config details.

## Mandatory Build Order

```
ThunderTools  →  Thunder  →  ThunderInterfaces  →  ThunderNanoServices / rdkservices
```

This order is **non-negotiable**. Each repo installs artifacts into a shared `install/` prefix that subsequent repos depend on. Attempting to build out of order produces missing-header or missing-generator errors.

## Shared Install Prefix

All repos must use the **same** `CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX`. In this workspace that is `${PWD}/install` (i.e., `/Users/.../ThunderBuild/install`). Never use `/usr/local` or any system prefix for development builds.

## Python venv Activation

ThunderTools generators are Python scripts. Activate the venv **before building any repo that triggers code generation**:

```bash
source /path/to/ThunderBuild/bin/activate
```

The venv must remain activated for the duration of the build session. Generation is triggered automatically by CMake when interface headers change.

## Step 1: ThunderTools

```bash
cmake -G Ninja -S ThunderTools -B build/ThunderTools \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="${PWD}/install"

cmake --build build/ThunderTools --target install
```

ThunderTools installs the Python-based generators into `install/`. No platform-specific flags are required.

## Step 2: Thunder (Core Daemon)

```bash
cmake -G Ninja -S Thunder -B build/Thunder \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="${PWD}/install" \
  -DTOOLS_SYSROOT="${PWD}/install" \
  -DBINDING="127.0.0.1" \
  -DPORT=55555 \
  -DINITV_SCRIPT=OFF \
  -DENABLED_TRACING_LEVEL=3 \
  -DHIDE_NON_EXTERNAL_SYMBOLS=ON \
  -DBUILD_REFERENCE=$(git -C Thunder rev-parse HEAD)

cmake --build build/Thunder --target install
```

## Step 3: ThunderInterfaces

```bash
cmake -G Ninja -S ThunderInterfaces -B build/ThunderInterfaces \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="${PWD}/install" \
  -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH="${PWD}/install"

cmake --build build/ThunderInterfaces --target install
```

## Step 4: ThunderNanoServices / rdkservices

```bash
# ThunderNanoServices
cmake -G Ninja -S ThunderNanoServices -B build/ThunderNanoServices \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="${PWD}/install" \
  -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH="${PWD}/install"

cmake --build build/ThunderNanoServices --target install

# rdkservices (same pattern)
cmake -G Ninja -S rdkservices -B build/rdkservices \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
  -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX="${PWD}/install" \
  -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH="${PWD}/install"

cmake --build build/rdkservices --target install
```

## Key CMake Options Reference

| Option | Default | Meaning |
|--------|---------|---------|
| `CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE` | `Release` | Use `Debug` for debug symbols, `MinSizeRel` for production |
| `HIDE_NON_EXTERNAL_SYMBOLS` | `OFF` | `ON` enables `-fvisibility=hidden` — **must** match production builds |
| `TOOLS_SYSROOT` | — | Path where ThunderTools generators are installed |
| `BINDING` | `0.0.0.0` | IP address Thunder binds JSON-RPC WebSocket server to |
| `PORT` | `80` | JSON-RPC WebSocket port |
| `BUILD_REFERENCE` | — | Git hash; surfaced via Controller `/status`; **must** be set in production |
| `ENABLED_TRACING_LEVEL` | `0` | Compile-time TRACE verbosity (0=off, 1=error, 2=warn, 3=info) |
| `WARNING_REPORTING` | `OFF` | Enable `REPORT_OUTOFBOUNDS_WARNING` / `REPORT_DURATION_WARNING` |
| `INITV_SCRIPT` | `ON` | Init script generation; set `OFF` for dev builds |
| `VIRTUALINPUT` | `ON` | Virtual input support; may be disabled on platforms without it |
| `PROTOCOLS` | — | Comma-separated list of enabled protocol plugins |

## Rebuilding a Single Repo (Without Clean)

```bash
cmake --build build/Thunder --target install
```

CMake + Ninja tracks dependencies — only changed files are rebuilt. This is the standard incremental build command.

## Clean Rebuild of a Single Repo

```bash
cmake --build build/Thunder --target clean
cmake --build build/Thunder --target install
```

To fully reconfigure (e.g., after changing CMake options):

```bash
rm -rf build/Thunder
cmake -G Ninja -S Thunder -B build/Thunder [... options ...]
cmake --build build/Thunder --target install
```

## Code Generation

Proxy/stub and JSON-RPC schema files are **auto-generated by CMake** when interface headers change. Triggers:
- Any change to an `interfaces/I*.h` file in `ThunderInterfaces/`
- Adding a new `@json` or `@stubgen:` annotation tag to a method

**Manual regeneration** (rarely needed, but available):
```bash
# Proxy/stub for a single interface
python3 install/bin/ProxyStubGenerator/StubGenerator.py \
  --include install/include \
  ThunderInterfaces/interfaces/IMyInterface.h

# JSON-RPC wrapper
python3 install/bin/JsonGenerator/JsonGenerator.py \
  --include install/include \
  ThunderInterfaces/interfaces/IMyInterface.h
```

Never hand-edit generated files. Edit the source interface header and rebuild.

## Running Thunder

```bash
install/bin/Thunder -c install/etc/Thunder/config.json
```

Additional useful flags:

| Flag | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `-c <path>` | Config file path (default: `/etc/Thunder/config.json`) |
| `-f` | Foreground mode (do not daemonize) |
| `-e <level>` | Override trace level at runtime |

Key fields in `config.json` that affect startup behavior: `port`, `binding`, `communicator`, `persistentpath`, `datapath`, `systempath`, `proxystubpath`, `configs` (plugin config directory).

## macOS-Specific Build Notes

- The same CMake commands work on macOS — no platform-specific wrapper scripts are needed.
- **`HIDE_NON_EXTERNAL_SYMBOLS=ON` is strongly recommended** for macOS builds — it applies `-fvisibility=hidden` which matches production Linux builds and exposes visibility-related bugs early.
- **Never pass `-flat_namespace`** to the macOS linker — it merges all symbol namespaces across all dylibs, breaking plugin isolation and causing unpredictable symbol conflicts.
- **RPATH setup** for macOS (add to Thunder CMake configure):
  ```bash
  -DCMAKE_INSTALL_RPATH="@loader_path;@loader_path/../lib" \
  -DCMAKE_BUILD_WITH_INSTALL_RPATH=ON
  ```
  This ensures dylibs resolve at runtime without needing `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH`.
- **`LD_LIBRARY_PATH` does not work on macOS** — use `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH` for one-off overrides, or set rpaths at build time (preferred).
- Proxy stubs and plugins use `.dylib` extension on macOS vs `.so` on Linux — CMake handles this automatically via `CMAKE_SHARED_LIBRARY_SUFFIX`.
- Apple Clang may emit additional warnings not seen on GCC: `-Wshorten-64-to-32`, `-Wunused-private-field`. Fix these rather than suppress them.

## Plugin Hot-Reload at Runtime

Plugin config JSON files in the `configs` directory are watched by `ConfigObserver`. Changes are applied without restarting the daemon:
- Plugin config file change → `Service::ConfigUpdated()` → plugin deactivated, reconfigured, re-activated (if autostart).
- Proxy stub `.so`/`.dylib` files dropped into `proxystubpath` are hot-loaded — new stubs are registered immediately.
- Proxy stubs are **never hot-unloaded** at runtime (unloading causes use-after-free if any call is in flight).

## CI Replication

To replicate CI builds locally, use `MinSizeRel` build type and ensure `HIDE_NON_EXTERNAL_SYMBOLS=ON` is set — CI catches visibility errors that debug builds hide.
